tasks.title: Freeze smart agent contract and regression guardrails
tasks.description: Document and enforce the public and behavioral contracts that must survive the refactor of smart_agent.py, including Agent/OmicVerseAgent entry points, streaming semantics, workflow linkage, and key downstream integrations. This task should add or tighten targeted regression coverage only where needed to make later extractions safe.
acceptance_criteria: A maintained contract matrix exists for public APIs and critical behaviors; targeted tests or equivalent regression checks cover Agent factory, run_async/stream_async, restart_session/get_session_history, workflow injection, and at least one downstream integration seam used by Jarvis or Claw.
tasks.interface_contracts: Agent(...), OmicVerseAgent(...), run_async(...), stream_async(...), restart_session(...), get_session_history(...), list_supported_models(...)
escalation_history: Blocked pending Taiwan-server validation path. Current ngagent review is configured for local pytest, but user requires validation on the Taiwan server. Investigate via task-007 before retrying task-001.

tasks.title: Extract bootstrap, auth, and runtime setup from smart_agent
tasks.description: Move credential resolution, auth mode normalization, backend setup, skill registry initialization, filesystem context instruction building, and related constructor bootstrapping out of smart_agent.py into focused runtime modules while preserving constructor behavior and backend selection semantics.
acceptance_criteria: OmicVerseAgent.__init__ no longer directly owns the bulk of bootstrap/auth/backend/skill/context setup; extracted modules have clear responsibilities; constructor call patterns and auth/backend behavior remain backward compatible; existing initialization and backend tests pass.
tasks.interface_contracts: Constructor compatibility for Agent(...) and OmicVerseAgent(...); backend/auth behavior for existing providers including OAuth-related flows

tasks.title: Extract code generation, review, and reflection pipeline
tasks.description: Move generate_code_async and its supporting code extraction, direct-python detection, review, and reflection helpers into a dedicated pipeline module so smart_agent.py delegates code-oriented flows instead of implementing them inline.
acceptance_criteria: The code generation path is owned by a dedicated module or service; smart_agent.py delegates rather than implementing extraction/review/reflection inline; code-only and execute_code-adjacent behavior remains compatible for current consumers including claw.py.
tasks.interface_contracts: generate_code_async behavior, code extraction expectations, review/reflection flow, and Claw-facing code-only semantics

tasks.title: Extract session, context, and history services
tasks.description: Move non-core orchestration helpers such as session reset/history access, temporary runtime context management, notebook/filesystem context helpers, and related service logic out of smart_agent.py into focused runtime services.
acceptance_criteria: Session and context lifecycle helpers are no longer scattered through smart_agent.py; restart_session and get_session_history preserve behavior; filesystem/notebook context handling is owned by dedicated service code with no change to public semantics.
tasks.interface_contracts: restart_session(...), get_session_history(...), session-scoped context behavior, and runtime/workflow context linkage

tasks.title: Consolidate tool facade and legacy tool exposure
tasks.description: Move or collapse legacy tool definitions, tool visibility filtering, plan-mode tool gating, and thin _tool_* wrappers so smart_agent.py no longer maintains a parallel tool facade on top of ToolRuntime. This task should preserve deferred tool loading and downstream tool contract behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tool schema visibility, legacy tool names, plan-mode restrictions, deferred loading, and wrapper behavior are preserved while smart_agent.py sheds duplicated tool-facade logic; ovagent/tool runtime becomes the clear source of truth for agent tool exposure.
tasks.interface_contracts: Visible tool schemas, legacy tool names, plan-mode blocking rules, deferred loading semantics, and downstream tool-call compatibility

tasks.title: Finalize smart_agent facade slimming and cleanup
tasks.description: Complete the refactor by removing obsolete compatibility clutter from smart_agent.py, aligning imports and composition around extracted ovagent services, and leaving a thin facade that cleanly delegates to the new module boundaries while preserving the public contract.
acceptance_criteria: smart_agent.py is reduced to a focused facade/composer with no duplicate execution subsystem implementations left behind; imports and module boundaries are coherent; public APIs and targeted regression checks continue to pass.
tasks.interface_contracts: Public facade surface remains stable while internal delegation becomes the primary implementation path

tasks.title: Establish Taiwan-server validation path for ngagent review
tasks.description: Design and implement the validation bridge required for this project so ngagent-managed tasks can be reviewed against the Taiwan server requirement. This includes deciding the remote repo or scratch path under /slow, the sync strategy from local worktrees, the reusable Python environment/bootstrap strategy, and the exact remote pytest entry points needed for smart-agent refactor tasks.
acceptance_criteria: A documented and executable Taiwan-server validation path exists for ngagent-managed worktrees; the remote host/path and operating rules are captured; the Python environment/bootstrap strategy is defined or provisioned; and the orchestrator has a concrete review procedure that does not rely on local-only pytest as final validation.
tasks.interface_contracts: Review/validation for this project must satisfy the Taiwan-server requirement while remaining compatible with ngagent task state transitions
